title: Mombasa’s old neighbourhoods prepare for a warmer tide
dek: Residents, architects and marine scientists are combining local knowledge with new coastal data.
author: Amina Salim
body: The sea has always set the rhythm of Mombasa. Now, higher tides and harder rain are changing where water collects and how long it remains.

Community maps are identifying the courtyards, lanes and drainage channels most at risk. Designers are testing restored mangroves, shaded public space and smaller interventions residents can maintain.
The work offers a lesson for coastal cities everywhere: adaptation succeeds when the people who know each street are part of designing its future.
